title: Retrieve Memories: - Recreate the Setting: Recall the Sights, Sounds, and Smells of the
subtitle: Use Context to Boost Recall
description:
To retrieve memories:
- Recreate the setting: Recall the sights, sounds, and smells of the original context.
- Use prompts: Bring objects or tools from the original environment to trigger memory.
- Mentally revisit: Imagine yourself back in the original situation.

Example: To recall work-related ideas, sit at your desk or review materials from the office.

howTo.title: Define the memory target
howTo.summary: Choose a specific memory you want to retrieve, such as a key idea or detail from a meeting. Clearly articulate this target to focus your retrieval efforts.

howTo.title: Gather sensory cues
howTo.summary: Identify and collect 2-3 sensory cues from the original context, including visual, auditory, and olfactory elements. These cues will help recreate the environment for better recall.

howTo.title: Conduct the retrieval session
howTo.summary: Set up your environment with the gathered cues, adopt a matching posture, and engage with the sensory elements. Spend a few minutes immersing yourself in the context before attempting to recall the memory.

howTo.title: Adjust and retry if needed
howTo.summary: If the memory does not surface, change one cue and try again. This could involve switching the audio or altering your posture to enhance recall chances.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and insights from the retrieval attempt. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/context-cue-memory-recall/.
